characters.name: Character appearances
characters.description: These charts list the appearances of all characters who have had significant recurring roles onSmallville(two or more episodes). This is the key for the colors used in an episode. Only one color can be used per episode. Photographs do not count as appearances. Character appears in episode, in live action with lines.

characters.name: Character of the week
characters.description: Lucas Luthor, son ofLioneland half-brother ofLex, is a normal human being and character of the week in episode 2x15. Ryan James, a boy having telepathic abilities, is character of the week in episodes 1x16 and 2x08. Kevin Grady, a benevolent metahuman, is the character of the week in episode 4x19.

characters.name: Double (concept)
characters.description: "Youknow how they say everyone in life has a double? I had to look twice."–Roy Rothman,Zero "Clark, that drawing looks exactly like you."–Lana Lang,Relic "Youbear a striking resemblance to yourgreat-aunt. Has anyone ever told you that?"–William Tate,Relic

characters.name: Freak of the week
characters.description: Jeremy Creek, first freak of the week at all, had been strung up as 'Scarecrow' when the1989 meteor showerhit Smallville in the show'spilot. Alicia Baker, freak of the week in episode 3x14 and 4x11, received her teleporting abilities in the1989 meteor shower. Sean Kelvin, freak of the week in episode 1x05, received his heat sucking powers when he drowned in a lake that was filled with meteor rocks.

characters.name: Gods
characters.description: GodsorDeitiesare higher beings, often featured in religions and worshipped by people. Over the years, the show established a number of religions (including Christianity,[1]Judaism,[2]Islam,[3]Hinduism,[4]Buddhism[5], and Native American mythology[6]), with deities fromEgyptianandGreekreligions even appearing onscreen, confirming the existence of the deities from those religions[7][8]However, the origin for all of these beings is unknown. The Mayan Rain god mask, Vessel of Water Stone

characters.name: Hero of the week
characters.description: Oliver QueenasGreen Arrow, a normal human acting as a hero. Arthur Curryas Aquaman, a metahuman super-hero. Hero of the weekreferred to episodes where aDC Comicssuperhero made a guest appearance inSmallville. The formula was first used inSeason Four.

characters.name: Kawatche
characters.description: TheKawatcheare a Native American tribe whose name literally means "Skinwalkers" and who possess an intimate link with the planetKrypton,The House of El, andClark Kentwhich stretches back over 500 years. Clark KentandKyla Willowbrookexplore theKawatche Caves. The oral history of the Kawatche people begins with a story of "a man from the stars" who came toEarthfive hundred years ago. This visitor fell in love with the"mother" of the Kawatche people. From that affair, according to legend, the tribe was born.

characters.name: Villain of the week
characters.description: Sam Phelan, a rogue cop appearing in episode 1x09, is the first villain of the week who is not ameteor freak. Rachel Dunleavy, mother ofLex's half-brotherLucas, appears in episode 2x07 as the first female villain of the week. Thedisciples of Zod, twoKryptonians, appear in episode 5x01 as the firstalienvillains of the week.
